column1: commentaire

1 star — completely unacceptable.Air Algérie diverted my flight from Heathrow to Stansted without notifying me at all. I only found out through a friend, not the airline. That alone says everything about their communication — or lack of it.I live near Heathrow, so being forced to travel an extra hour to Stansted was a major disruption. Worse still, I was traveling with my special needs daughter who requires a wheelchair. The airline showed zero consideration for passengers with disabilities or families with additional needs. Getting to Stansted is a serious challenge for us.No warning, no assistance, no empathy. This experience was stressful, exhausting, and completely avoidable. I would strongly advise disabled passengers and families to avoid Air Algérie.
Why have an online check in if I have to check in again at the airport? Wtf is wrong with you?

My checked bag went missing after my flight with Air Algérie, and after some time the airline told me that it had been found at Béjaïa Airport. They said it would be sent to my home address, since I had already left Algeria. After that message, everything stopped.I have been trying to contact Air Algérie since the summer — emails, forms, messages — and I have not received a single reply. No update about my bag, no explanation, no follow-up. Months have passed and the airline continues to ignore every attempt I make to resolve the situation.Losing a bag can happen with any airline, but the complete silence after confirming they had located it is unacceptable. My belongings have still not been returned, and I have had to deal with this entirely on my own.This has been one of the worst customer service experiences I have ever had, and I am sharing this so others are aware of how Air Algérie handles situations when something goes wrong.
Worst customer service in this world, which kind of people who you can hear them talking bad about the client..
The worst airline you can ever imagine. Changed my flight and was refused refund, not even a partial refund. Avoid at all cost.

Honestly a disaster!! I haven't used Air Algerie for over 15 years. I gave it ago today, and it was a catastrophic experience from start to end. Today the flight was on time, however, 1st class no signs or any indications to board as 1st class, we only been instructed to line up all in one lane and aggressive behaviour from the old lady leading the team, I hesitated to ask, but than had to and as the queue was too long etc... I was told yeah its ok to jump the queue but no organisation or anythjng... But importantly is once again the under performance of the staff not qualified to do the job to my opinion in all areas, it's obvious they are not scared of getting disciplined or reprimanded they make you feel they own the place and us customers we are under valued in fact we are nothing! The plane was very poor standards very old dirty everywhere and the shock is with all honesty from the start soon as we boarded the guy in charge Sat on a chair had coffee and kept talking to a couple of stewards they go n come back than that corner right behind the co pit turned into a meeting room they were 4 of them sat talking even when the pilot announced we are descending where the procedure is to raise the curtains and staff to lead by example after checking passengers and seat belt etc no no no one was checking they put the curtain down so that they dont get disturbed kept talking until nearly the end... It was disrespectful and unprofessional! I will never ever use Air Algerie again simple! Today's flight Algiers to London Heathrow....

If there was a way to give this airline a minus  multiple times I would, how can you keep someone for almost a day without provision of accommodation or food, then when she complains you block her!Meanwhile your white passengers are treated like VIPs!!!!!!!!!!!!

I am writing to express my deep dissatisfaction with the treatment my mother and I received during a recent flight check-in experience with your airline.My mother, an elderly and disabled woman who requires a wheelchair, was subjected to unnecessary stress and humiliation at the check-in counter. Her passport includes the abbreviation “Vve” to indicate that she is a widow, as is standard in French documentation. Unfortunately, your staff seemed completely unprepared to understand or process this minor detail. They questioned the validity of her passport over those three small letters and nearly denied her boarding as a result.Despite my calm attempts to explain that “Vve” simply stands for “veuve,” meaning “widow,” the agent showed little understanding or empathy. This situation alone was distressing enough for my mother, who cannot stand for extended periods and was visibly uncomfortable.To make matters worse, another staff member came over and, in an extremely disrespectful manner, instructed my mother to sit on the luggage scale. Asking an elderly woman to sit on a scale in full view of other passengers is not only inappropriate but also deeply humiliating. This completely disregarded her dignity.The same staff then insisted on taking a photo of my mother’s passport. I explained that this was sensitive personal information and that such an action was inappropriate. The agent acknowledged my concern but still refused to proceed without taking the photo.This entire ordeal was distressing, unprofessional, and unacceptable. My mother was left in tears, and I was shocked at how your staff handled the situation.I must ask: Is this how Air Algeria treats elderly and disabled passengers? Why are staff members not trained to recognize common international document notations, or to handle such matters with care and respect?

The worse Airlines I have ever come across,  almost all thier, flights delayed, up to 4hrs delay at transit without any compensation and my luggages where left behind on both trips,  which I only received it after 2 weeks,  one of bags was destroyed and I lost valuables inside which I wrote the customer service on several occasions but have never gotten a reply 😢
Good in Algérie

The worst company ever. I bought my ticket with 19000 Da Istanbul back to Algeria due my researches I wanted to delay my trip back home until summer and as my family reached them, they said I need to pay 50 euros plus 80000 da ( 8 million avec notre derja) this doesn't make a sense by any means! This amount could be a round trip even ! How can you tell me that I would be able to change only within the limit of two months and I should make it open ( and even the programmed trips if I pay that amount is only till the month of march)?? When was this regulation of expensive extra fees created and why your customer service don't tell us abt these situations in advance??

I booked a trip from Paris to Johannesburg via Last Minute Booking - Air Algerie cancelled the flight 2 weeks before the flight. I have not received my refund and its 4 months later; Last Minute keeps putting the blame on Air Algerie, as they have not refunded them. DO NOT USE THIS AIRLINE. I've rated both companies 1 star - because wow, what is this service. Based on their reviews I don't understand how they even have 2 stars on here.
